title: Creamy Canadian Duck Tagine
description: A romantic Canadian dish featuring tender duck and a rich, peppery sauce, cooked to perfection in a tagine with Shaoxing wine, and finished with a glassy jelly glaze.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
Season the duck breasts with black pepper.
Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat and sear the duck breasts until browned on both sides.
Transfer the duck breasts to a tagine or a large Dutch oven.
In a small bowl, whisk together the Shaoxing wine, low-sodium chicken broth, honey, garlic, and ginger.
Pour the wine mixture over the duck breasts in the tagine.
Cover the tagine and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Braise the duck for 2 hours, or until tender and cooked through.
In a small bowl, whisk together the cornstarch and a splash of the cooking liquid until smooth.
Stir the cornstarch mixture back into the tagine and continue to cook for an additional 10 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened.
Stir in the chopped green onions and serve the duck breasts with the rich, peppery sauce spooned over the top.
